description: correction: C1-52WH-0001 result is COMPROMISED by a price-panel data defect found in post-run audit
notes: DEFECT: 33 dates in the panel carried yfinance holiday-placeholder rows (open==high==low==close, volume 0) for only 4 symbols (CAPF, DALMIABHA, INFINITE, POLARIS) - 132 rows, 0.005% of 2.48M. Pivoting to wide turns each into a NaN row for every OTHER symbol, and expr._rolling_max uses min_periods=252, so one NaN blocks nearness for the whole trailing window. Last bad date 2017-10-20 pushed first defined nearness to 2018-10-26 instead of 2016-01-08. CONSEQUENCE: walk-forward started 2018-04-02 not ~2016, and the first 3 rebalances (2018-04-02, 2018-07-02, 2018-10-01) ran with ranked=0, i.e. NO screening - the two arms were the identical book for the first ~6 months, including the opening leg of the 2018-2020 drawdown the spec requires be spanned. Reported headline (screened net IR +0.349 vs unscreened +0.181, increment +0.168, SPA p 0.034, turnover 76.2%/yr, verdict SURVIVES) must NOT be cited. SEPARATE and independent of the defect: DSR was 0.025 against sr0 1.146 - i.e. even at face value the active Sharpe 0.349 sits far below the best-of-52-trials selection threshold; the spec §8 kill line tests only IR>0 and SPA p, so SURVIVES understates how weak this is. FIXES (committed): scripts/build_price_panel.py::drop_non_trading_dates quorum filter; backtest_52wh raises on signal starvation (ranked/tradeable < min_ranked_frac) so this class of silent no-op is now a hard stop; regression tests both. Trial count NOT reclaimed - C1-52WH-0001 is spent. A corrected re-run is a NEW trial requiring a new pre-registered row; NOT yet authorized.

description: Opening-bar volume share s = first_5min_volume / full_day_volume on up-sessions, 250-name system universe (RULING 13)
notes: PRE-REGISTERED BEFORE OUTCOME CONTACT. Purpose: measure s to test whether preopen_check.py's hardcoded x75 (uniform-intraday-volume assumption) miscalibrates the 1.0x/1.2x go-no-go gate; 1/mean(s) is the empirically correct multiplier. PARAMETERS FIXED HERE, NOT TUNABLE AFTER: universe = 250 names from data/sealed/raw/2026-07-22/prices_eod_backup_20260624.csv (nifty50+niftynext50+niftymidcap150 deduped, config.py:30); window = yfinance 60-day 5m horizon approx 2026-05-23 to 2026-07-22 (~40 sessions; deeper history needs Kite = BINDING RULE 3, refused); up-session filter = close of 15:25-15:30 bar > close of 09:15-09:20 bar; denominator = yfinance daily volume (prices_eod semantics), fallback intraday sum; primary estimator = volume-weighted mean of s with weights = full_day_volume (equals pooled sum(first5)/sum(full_day)); guards = placeholder rows (flat OHLC + zero volume) dropped, bar quorum 70 of 75, 09:15 bar located by IST clock time not position, s>1 dropped as basis mismatch. Reported alongside: unweighted mean, median, decile spread, all-sessions control, liquidity-quintile breakdown. Trial charged against LEGACY family (already killed, no sealed test at risk); NO shadow slot consumed; FINAL_TEST NOT set. Cumulative trial count = 53. Runner: scripts/diag_open_volume_share.py. MEASUREMENT ONLY - RULING 13d forbids changing the 75 or the 1.2 before the 2026-09-27 AB_PREREG read date.

description: Result of DIAG-VOLSHARE-0001 (scripts/diag_open_volume_share.py) — opening-bar volume share s, 250-name universe
result: CONFIRMED: x75 overstates by 3.1x-4.4x; gate near-vacuous
notes: COVERAGE: 250/250 symbols (100%), 14,197 clean symbol-sessions, 57 trading dates 2026-04-30 to 2026-07-22, 6,572 up-sessions analysed; dropped 140 placeholder bars, 20 sub-quorum sessions, 32 implausible residuals, 1 missing 09:15 bar. RESULT: s (first-5-min share of daily volume, up-sessions) = 4.14% median / 5.82% volume-weighted (operator estimator) / 4.81% unweighted mean; decile spread 1.64%-8.78%. Versus the 1/75 = 1.33% implicitly assumed by preopen_check.py, i.e. x75 OVERSTATES the opening bar's representativeness by 3.10x (median) to 4.36x (vol-wtd). Empirically correct multiplier is 17-24, not 75. CONSEQUENCE: the 1.2x volume-confirmation gate in synthesis.py actually demands only 0.28x-0.39x of 20-day average volume, and the 1.0x line only 0.23x-0.32x - i.e. the gate passes everything except unusually QUIET days. By liquidity quintile s is 3.51% (thinnest) to 5.16% (deepest), monotone-ish, so no single global constant is correct. METHOD: residual, s = 1 - (sum of 5m bars / official daily volume), because yfinance reports ZERO volume for the 09:15-09:20 bar in 97.65% of sessions while carrying its correct price (bar labelling verified START-labelled: 09:15 bar open == official NSE open, ADANIPORTS 2026-06-24 = 1786.00 exactly). Denominator validated: yfinance daily volume vs official prices_eod backup 2026-06-24 agrees within 0.01%. INDEPENDENT CROSS-CHECK: on the 333 sessions (214 distinct symbols) where yfinance DID report the opening volume, DIRECT s = 4.07% median vs 4.14% residual-inferred - agreement to 0.07pp validates the method. ESTIMATOR CORRECTION made BEFORE this result was recorded: the tail (closing auction + off-book) was first estimated by volume-weighted mean = 4.22%, which is outlier-inflated (a few sessions up to 27%); the median tail is 0.02%, i.e. ~zero. Using the weighted tail subtracted a phantom and collapsed s to 1.62%, spuriously near 1/75 - caught in audit, script fixed to use the median. CAVEATS: s is an estimate over ~57 sessions in one regime, not a constant; universe membership is the 2026-06-24 snapshot applied to the whole window; window contains expiry days. INCIDENTAL FINDING (clone-derived, RULING 9 requires a production HEAD check before acting): preopen_check.py _yf_first_candle locates the 09:15 bar by IST clock time and computes vol*75/avg_vol, so on yfinance data vol=0 gives volume_ratio=0.0, which synthesis.py reads as below-1.0x -> 'unconfirmed breakout' on EVERY stock, conviction capped 60, top5 downgraded to borderline, whenever the Kite path is unavailable. RULING 13d: MEASUREMENT ONLY - no live parameter change before the 2026-09-27 AB_PREREG read date.

description: refinement: s reconstructed from production's OWN Kite-sourced volume_ratio (22 rec-days) — sharpens DIAG-VOLSHARE-0001-RESULT
result: NO TRIAL SPENT (outcome-blind: volume only, no outcome conditioning)
notes: METHOD: recorded volume_ratio from data/sealed/raw/*/candle_verdicts_*.json (Kite-sourced, the live gate's own input) inverted via vol_first5 = volume_ratio * avg20 / 75, with avg20 = mean daily volume over the 20 sessions STRICTLY BEFORE the rec date (matching get_avg_volume_20d, which reads prices_eod holding data through the prior session) and daily volume from the yfinance cache validated to 0.01% against the official prices_eod backup. 22/22 rec-days reconstructable, all passing 0<s<1. FOUR INDEPENDENT ROUTES NOW CONVERGE on s (median): 4.14% yfinance residual (n=6,572 up-sessions), 4.07% yfinance direct (n=333 sessions / 214 symbols), ~4.05% Kite ratio-inference, 3.94% Kite exact reconstruction (n=22). Two routes are yfinance intraday and two are production Kite, so agreement is not a shared-source artefact. HEADLINE: s ~ 4.0% vs the 1.33% implied by x75; correct multiplier ~25 not 75; overstatement ~2.95x. CORRECTS the original result row in two respects: (1) the VOLUME-WEIGHTED estimator (originally reported as the operator-specified headline, 5.82% on yfinance data) is UNSTABLE at small n and must not be used here - the reconstruction's volume-weighted figure is 8.64%, dominated by a single observation (KALYANKJIL 2026-07-14, volume_ratio 28.54 at 3.62x relative volume, probable news event); the MEDIAN is the only stable statistic. Same outlier-sensitivity that produced the withdrawn 4.22% phantom tail earlier in this study. (2) the original 'near-vacuous gate' characterisation OVERSTATED: recorded ratios show the gates reject 13.6% (1.0x), 27.3% (1.5x) and 36.4% (2.0x) of candidates, so they bind - they are MISLABELLED, not inert. At 2.95x overstatement the nominal 1.0x/1.5x/2.0x gates really demand 0.34x/0.51x/0.68x of the 20-day average. RESOLVED ASSUMPTION: rec days do NOT run systematically hot - relative volume (rec-day / avg20) median 1.00x, mean 1.17x, range 0.38-3.62x - so no hot-day correction is needed and the 'normal volume' branch of the earlier bracket is correct. PER-STOCK SPREAD IS ~10x (s from 1.14% NAUKRI to 10.51% KALYANKJIL): AJANTPHARM at s=2.61% has its nominal 1.0x gate really demanding 0.51x (it came in at 0.40x and was correctly rejected) while ABB at s=10.19% has the SAME nominal gate demanding only 0.13x. NO single global constant serves both, so 75->25 would relocate the error rather than remove it; the per-symbol denominator (compare the opening bar to the 20-day average of that symbol's OWN opening bar, making s cancel) remains the recommended fix. ALSO CONFIRMED: zero instances of volume_ratio == 0.0 and zero None across all 22 rec-days, i.e. the yfinance-fallback all-veto failure mode has NOT fired in the live window - it is latent, not active, and its urgency is downgraded accordingly. RULING 13d still binds: measurement only, no live parameter change before the 2026-09-27 AB_PREREG read date.

description: Cross-sectional macro-factor beta structure: market-adjusted loadings of 1,310 NSE names on Brent crude and USD/INR, dev window 2015-01-01..2024-07-16, scored against a hash-pinned a-priori economic sign map
notes: PRE-REGISTERED BEFORE ANY OUTCOME CONTACT (TRAP 3). NO TRIAL SPENT (outcome-blind: correlation structure only, no outcome conditioning) - CONTAMINATION_POLICY AMENDMENT A lists 'correlation and cointegration structure' as explicitly free. NOT a spec family, consumes NO shadow slot, and must not become one. FINAL_TEST NOT set; research door only. HARD BOUNDARY ACCEPTED: no portfolio construction, no strategy/spread return, no cumulative performance, no Sharpe/IR, no forward-return-conditional statistic, no outcome-conditional subsetting of any kind. Only betas, t-stats and their cross-sectional distribution. PURPOSE: test whether individual Indian equities carry a direct, persistent, MARKET-ADJUSTED loading on (a) crude oil and (b) USD/INR, and score the empirically identified set against an a-priori economic map fixed in advance. || PARAMETERS FIXED HERE, NOT TUNABLE AFTER || UNIVERSE: every symbol in data/workspace/price_panel_52wh.parquet (1,310 adjusted-OHLC names) that clears a pure data-availability filter of >=750 usable daily return observations in the full window; no other screen. Universe inherits TWO DISCLOSED DEFECTS that must appear in the write-up: (i) the AMFI blank-symbol habitat defect (OPEN OPERATOR DECISION 2) - 50,505 rank rows carry a blank symbol, so the 201-1000 band held 719 names not ~800 and ~10% of the intended habitat never reached the fetch list; (ii) the survivorship hole - 102/1,412 (7.2%) symbols yfinance could not serve, skewed toward delistings. DATE WINDOW: 2015-01-01 to 2024-07-16 inclusive (research door, seal cutoff 2024-07-17). TRADING CALENDAR: the panel's own dates after build_price_panel.drop_non_trading_dates (quorum 0.10) - the TRAP 1 guard; expected surviving session count asserted in [2300, 2380] and the run FAILS LOUDLY otherwise. FACTOR TICKERS (yfinance, BINDING RULE 3 compliant): crude primary Brent BZ=F; crude robustness WTI CL=F; FX primary INR=X; FX cross-check USDINR=X (the two must agree to corr>0.99 on log returns or the run halts); market NIFTY500 TRI from data/reference/tri/raw/nifty500_tri.csv. DERIVED FACTOR: INR-crude = BZ=F x USDINR, whose log return is r_brent + r_usdinr, run as a cross-check specification because an Indian firm's true crude exposure is to INR-denominated crude. ALIGNMENT: factor CLOSE levels are reindexed onto the NSE session calendar by EXACT DATE MATCH ONLY (no forward fill), then log-differenced between consecutive NSE sessions, so factor and stock returns span the identical interval; a session missing either endpoint is dropped listwise. Sensitivity: level ffill(limit=3), reported as the correlation between the two beta vectors. Overlap asserted at >=90% of NSE sessions; run FAILS LOUDLY otherwise. RETURNS: natural log returns throughout, never levels (levels would be a spurious regression). No cointegration claim is made or tested here. NO return-magnitude filtering in the primary estimate (that would be a return-conditional filter); |r|>0.25 and |r|>0.693 counts are merely REPORTED, and the Huber companion below handles influence. REGRESSION SPECIFICATION (joint, market control MANDATORY - two univariate correlations would 'discover' that nearly everything loads on crude, because in any risk-off episode crude falls, INR weakens and every stock falls together; that is one common factor, not hundreds of crude-sensitive stocks): r_i,t = alpha + b_mkt*r_mkt + b_oil*r_brent + b_fx*r_usdinr + e, per symbol, OLS. LAG STRUCTURE - three pre-registered specs, all reported: SPEC-C all regressors at t (contemporaneous; NSE closes 15:30 IST while Brent and USDINR trade on afterwards, so this is knowingly NON-SYNCHRONOUS); SPEC-L1 market at t, oil and fx at t-1 (residual content of yesterday's factor move after today's market) - PRIMARY LAGGED SPEC; SPEC-L2 all regressors at t-1 (pure lag). The contemporaneous-vs-lagged GAP is itself a pre-registered finding, not a nuisance. STANDARD ERRORS: Newey-West HAC, Bartlett kernel, lag 5, on every reported t-stat. Naive OLS t-stats are reported nowhere. ROBUST COMPANION (TRAP 8 - compute the robust estimator ALONGSIDE the primary and reconcile, do not merely comply): Huber M-estimate by IRLS, c=1.345 on MAD-scaled residuals, same design matrix, 50 iterations max, reported next to OLS for every FDR survivor and summarised cross-sectionally. Disagreement in SIGN between OLS and Huber disqualifies a name from the 'stable' list. MULTIPLE COMPARISONS (the central statistical hazard here: ~1,310 symbols x 2 factors ~ 2,600 tests, so ~130 false positives at p<0.05 by chance alone): Benjamini-Hochberg FDR at q=0.10, applied PER FACTOR PER SPEC as the primary family definition, with a pooled BH over both factors within a spec reported as the conservative variant. The FULL cross-sectional t-stat distribution against its theoretical N(0,1) null is reported; no cherry-picked top-N table is reported without it. The chance-expected discovery count is stated explicitly alongside every discovery count. SUB-PERIODS (boundaries fixed here): P1 2015-01-01..2017-12-31, P2 2018-01-01..2019-12-31, P3 2020-01-01..2021-12-31, P4 2022-01-01..2024-07-16. Sign stability requires >=120 observations in EVERY sub-period. A name is 'STABLE' only if it (a) survives BH q=0.10 in the full window, (b) carries the same beta sign in all four sub-periods, and (c) agrees in sign between OLS and Huber. SHOCK-EXCLUSION ROBUSTNESS: re-estimate SPEC-C dropping EX-COVID 2020-02-01..2020-06-30, EX-UKRAINE 2022-02-01..2022-06-30, and EX-BOTH; report the coefficient shift. A beta manufactured by a handful of extreme observations must be visible as such. FACTOR ENTANGLEMENT: corr(r_brent, r_usdinr), corr with r_mkt, and the full factor correlation matrix are reported, with an explicit statement of what the joint regression does and does not separate. THEORY SCORECARD - A-PRIORI EXPECTED SIGN MAP FIXED AND HASH-PINNED BEFORE CONTACT: governance/prereg/DIAG-MACROBETA-0001_sign_map.csv, sha256 16693972a15f72379049a84b76c508b8488740abe89f6120514f9ed3ddd49859, 127 symbols, 52 directional crude predictions and 76 directional USDINR predictions (generator scripts/prereg_macro_beta_map.py). SUMMARY OF THE MAP: CRUDE-POSITIVE = upstream E&P (ONGC, OIL - realisation IS crude, flagged as partly a policy beta via subsidy-sharing and the 2022 windfall levy), standalone refiners (CHENNPETRO, MRPL - GRM plus inventory gain), COALINDIA (energy substitution, weak). CRUDE-NEGATIVE = aviation (INDIGO, JETAIRWAYS - ATF 30-40% of opex), paints (ASIANPAINT, BERGEPAINT, KANSAINER, AKZOINDIA), tyres (MRF, APOLLOTYRE, CEATLTD, JKTYRE, BALKRISIND - carbon black + synthetic rubber), adhesives (PIDILITIND - VAM), plastics/packaging/pipes (SUPREMEIND, ASTRAL, UFLEX, COSMOFIRST, JINDALPOLY, TIMETECHNO, NILKAMAL, VIPIND, POLYPLEX), lubricants (CASTROLIND, GULFOILLUB - base oil), OMCs (IOC, BPCL, HINDPETRO - EXPLICITLY A POLICY BETA: administered/frozen retail pricing in 2017-18 and 2021-22 means margin compresses as crude rises; PRE-STATED RISK of sub-period sign instability), city gas and LNG (IGL, MGL, GUJGASLTD, PETRONET), cement (pet coke + diesel freight), autos (running-cost demand drag), FMCG (LAB + packaging, weak). USDINR-POSITIVE (stock rises as the rupee DEPRECIATES) = IT services (TCS, INFY, WIPRO, HCLTECH, TECHM, LTIM, MPHASIS, PERSISTENT, COFORGE - the strongest a-priori claim in the study; if IT does not load positively the METHOD is suspect, not the economics), pharma exporters (17 names, weaker than IT because imported KSM/API and USD acquisition debt offset), textile exporters (WELSPUNIND, TRIDENT, KPRMILL, VTL, ARVIND), low-USD-debt metal exporters (NATIONALUM, HINDZINC, NMDC), POLYPLEX (overseas subsidiaries), gold-loan NBFCs (MUTHOOTFIN, MANAPPURAM, weak). USDINR-NEGATIVE = aviation (INDIGO, JETAIRWAYS - USD fuel, leases and maintenance: predicted NEGATIVE ON BOTH FACTORS, the sharpest joint call in the map), paints (imported TiO2/monomers), OMCs, city gas and LNG, consumer durables and EMS (DIXON, AMBER, HAVELLS, VOLTAS, BLUESTARCO, CROMPTON, WHIRLPOOL, ORIENTELEC, BAJAJELEC, SYMPHONY), wires and cables (POLYCAB, KEI - LME copper in USD, domestic INR sales), telecom USD debt (BHARTIARTL, IDEA), TATAPOWER (imported coal + USD debt), domestic-skewed tyres, lubricants. TWO DELIBERATE DISCRIMINATING TESTS embedded in the map: BALKRISIND is predicted crude-NEG but FX-POSITIVE (export off-highway tyres) where MRF/APOLLOTYRE/CEATLTD/JKTYRE are FX-NEGATIVE; BAJAJ-AUTO is FX-POSITIVE (~40% export) where HEROMOTOCO carries no FX prediction. A map that cannot separate these is not doing work. EXPLICITLY AMBIGUOUS AND EXCLUDED FROM HIT-RATE SCORING (their pre-registered prediction is 'no reliable sign'): RELIANCE (refining+petchem vs Jio/Retail dilution - PRE-STATED as a sign-changer within the window), high-USD-debt metals (HINDALCO, TATASTEEL, JSWSTEEL, VEDL), the entire specialty-chemicals bucket (SRF, AARTIIND, ATUL, DEEPAKNTR, NOCIL, VINATIORGA, FLUOROCHEM, NAVINFLUOR, PIIND, TATACHEM - cost-plus pass-through plus export books make both signs defensible; unscoring them is the point, since the alternative is an unfalsifiable map in which every chemical name confirms the theory), jewellery (TITAN, KALYANKJIL, RAJESHEXPO), the Adani names (Jan-2023 Hindenburg dominates their variance), shipping (GESHIP, SCI), GRASIM. SCORING RULES FIXED HERE: (1) HIT RATE = among map symbols carrying a directional prediction on a factor AND surviving BH q=0.10 on that factor in SPEC-C, the fraction whose beta sign matches the prediction; (2) COVERAGE = fraction of directional map symbols that survive BH at all; (3) SIGN TEST = one-sided binomial test of sign-agreement versus p=0.5 across ALL directional map symbols regardless of significance, which is the primary validation statistic because it does not condition on discovery; (4) SURPRISES = FDR survivors in the top decile of |t| that carry NO map entry, enumerated with sector, as prime suspects for spurious correlation; (5) MISSES = directional map symbols that fail to survive, enumerated, as evidence AGAINST the mechanism. All five are reported whatever they show. PRE-STATED ACCEPTABLE OUTCOME: 'no stock shows a stable, market-adjusted, FDR-surviving macro beta' is a complete and valuable result and will be reported as the headline if that is what the data says. No positive result will be manufactured. PRE-STATED CONCEPTUAL CAVEAT (to appear prominently in the write-up, not buried): a contemporaneous beta is an EXPOSURE, not an alpha. Even a rock-solid crude beta is untradable unless crude itself is predictable - a separate and much harder claim this diagnostic does NOT address and is NOT authorized to address. Legitimate uses are risk management, hedging, factor-neutralisation and conditioning for someone who already holds a macro view. Most such betas are long known and priced. OUTPUTS: data/derived/macro_beta_diag/ (per-symbol betas, factor cache, coverage report); analysis/macro_beta_diagnostic.md. Runner: scripts/diag_macro_beta.py --trial-id DIAG-MACROBETA-0001. Cumulative trial count UNCHANGED at 53.

description: Result of DIAG-MACROBETA-0001 (scripts/diag_macro_beta.py) - crude beta structure VALIDATED (sign test 76.9%, p=6.4e-5; paints NEG + E&P POS survive FDR+stability+shock); USD/INR WEAK/horizon-limited (59%, p=0.068; pharma-export cluster real but sub-FDR; IT-services the pre-registered strongest claim MISSES at daily frequency)
result: COMPLETE: crude exposure real & economically coherent; USDINR weak; NO TRIAL SPENT
notes: NO TRIAL SPENT (outcome-blind: correlation/beta structure only, no outcome conditioning) - cumulative trial count UNCHANGED at 53. Runner scripts/diag_macro_beta.py; artifacts data/derived/macro_beta_diag/; write-up analysis/macro_beta_diagnostic.md. A-priori map sha256 16693972a15f72379049a84b76c508b8488740abe89f6120514f9ed3ddd49859 (unchanged post-contact, TRAP 3). COVERAGE (read first, TRAP 2): all trust guards PASSED - 2353 NSE sessions (band [2300,2380]) after dropping 33 holiday-placeholder dates; corr(INR=X, USDINR=X) log-ret = 1.00000; factor overlaps BZ=F 96.86% / CL=F 96.81% / INR=X 99.79% / NIFTY500 TRI 100%; 1130 of 1309 symbols cleared the >=750-obs availability filter, median n=2199. Contemporaneous corr(r_brent, r_usdinr) = 0.057 so the two factor betas are cleanly separable; corr(r_usdinr, r_mkt) = -0.139 (the risk-off confound the mandatory market control removes). SPEC: r_i = a + b_mkt*r_mkt + b_oil*r_brent + b_fx*r_usdinr, log returns, Newey-West lag5 t-stats, Huber (c=1.345) companion, BH FDR q=0.10; three lag specs C/L1/L2. HEADLINE - CRUDE: a direct, market-adjusted, economically coherent cross-sectional exposure EXISTS and is validated. Sign test (does NOT condition on discovery) = 40/52 = 76.9% a-priori-sign agreement, one-sided binomial p=6.4e-5. SPEC-C BH q=0.10 gives 6 crude survivors (vs ~56 chance FPs at p<.05, 77 observed); 4 are map-directional and ALL 4 match sign: ASIANPAINT b=-0.080 t=-6.66, BERGEPAINT -0.089 t=-5.16, PIDILITIND -0.064 t=-4.24 (crude-derived inputs), DALBHARAT -0.090 t=-3.77 (cement, but a COVID artifact - flips to +0.009 ex-both-shocks). STABLE set (BH + 4-subperiod sign-consistent + OLS/Huber agree): ASIANPAINT, BERGEPAINT (NEG, paints) and E&P surprises HINDOILEXP +0.110 t+3.86, VEDL +0.086 t+3.94 (POS, realisation channel; both shock-robust). Primary lagged SPEC-L1 WIDENS crude to 16 survivors (paints + ONGC/OIL/HINDOILEXP/ABAN/GAIL/HINDPETRO + oil-metals VEDL/HINDALCO/TATASTEEL/JINDALSTEL/JINDRILL + tyres CEATLTD/MRF): the lagged crude beta is STRONGER and BROADER than contemporaneous, the expected non-synchronous-timing signature (NSE closes 15:30 IST before Brent settles) and itself a pre-registered finding (C-vs-L1 crude-beta corr 0.24). ONGC +0.082 t+2.20, OIL +0.052, COALINDIA +0.036, INDIGO -0.080 t-2.98 (aviation/ATF) all match. HEADLINE - USD/INR: WEAK and horizon-limited. Sign test 45/76 = 59.2%, p=0.068 - directional but not significant at 0.05. ZERO map-directional names survive BH at daily frequency (C_fx survivors=2, both SURPRISES outside the map: SKFINDIA -0.35 t-3.86 sensible FX-neg, IPAPPM +0.61 t+3.81 thin paper, spurious-suspect). The one genuine cluster is PHARMA EXPORTERS, all POSITIVE as predicted and the largest FX loadings in the map (SUNPHARMA +0.385 t+3.32, AUROPHARMA +0.448 t+3.10, BIOCON +0.304 t+2.80, LUPIN t+2.31, IPCALAB t+2.12, CIPLA t+1.88) - real in AGGREGATE, individually just below the FDR bar (p~0.0009 vs required ~0.0003). PRE-REGISTERED HEADLINE MISS: IT services, the map's single strongest a-priori claim, does NOT show at daily frequency (TCS -0.105 t-1.21, WIPRO -0.120 t-1.50 both weakly NEG; mixed signs, all |t|<1.6). Honest reading: not a broken method (crude validated on the same pipeline) but a horizon effect - IT hedges 1-2 quarters forward, so daily spot USD/INR is muted; the exposure is quarterly, invisible to a daily beta. Caution against the naive 'IT = long USDINR' day-trade. DISCRIMINATING TESTS (mixed): BALKRISIND FX-POS +0.194 right direction but weak t+1.47 and MRF came out FX-POS despite NEG prediction, so the export/domestic tyre split only half-fired; BAJAJ-AUTO FX prediction FAILED (-0.084, wrong sign). MARKET-CONTROL LESSON MADE VISIBLE: SPEC-L2 (market ALSO lagged) inflates the whole cross-section (t_mean +1.097, 32 'survivors') because contemporaneous market variance is uncontrolled - shown only to demonstrate why the market control is mandatory; L1 (market at t) is the primary lagged spec. DISCLOSED DEFECTS: inherited AMFI habitat defect (~10% narrow, power loss only, no directional bias); survivorship hole 7.2% skewed to delistings BIASES AGAINST finding crude-/FX-negative names (distress correlates with leverage and input-cost shocks), i.e. the true crude signal is if anything stronger than measured - conservative for the verdict. CAVEAT (prominent): a contemporaneous beta is an EXPOSURE not an alpha; untradable unless crude itself is predictable (a separate, harder, UNADDRESSED and unauthorized claim). Legitimate uses = risk management, hedging, factor-neutralisation, conditioning for an existing macro view. Most of these betas are long known and priced. Any 'would it have made money' question is a registered TRIAL outside this authorization.

description: Post-breakout entry feasibility (operator question 2026-07-23): can entering right after a confirmed breakout capture remaining upside net of friction at Rs100Cr?
notes: OUTCOME-BLIND, NO TRIAL SPENT, NO SHADOW SLOT, no repo outcome data touched, no spec drafted. Analysis: analysis/breakout_entry_feasibility.md. Finding: the idea is the legacy system's own volume-confirmed-52wk-high entry rule (killed: gross 18.5%/yr ~= statutory cost 19.7%/yr at 1.6 RT/day); friction hurdle needs +42-222 bps gross drift per round trip (costs_in.py, RULING 5); capacity fails at Rs100Cr by ~an order of magnitude (SRA-shaped); literature's only India-replicated near-high effect is the negative-screen SHORT leg (Raju 2023, long leg t=-0.91), short-horizon documented sign is REVERSAL; volume-confirmation gate itself mis-calibrated ~2.95x (RULING 13). Surviving shape = SPEC-52WH-01 negative screen, already frozen+queued. GOVERNANCE NOTE: SWEEP_HOLDING.md outcome table (quarantined per DECISIONS.md 2026-07-20 VARIANT-A) was inadvertently read this session and disclosed (see DECISIONS.md 2026-07-23 INCIDENT); NOT used in this analysis.

description: Feasibility/scoping of a crude-oil directional forecast engine chained onto the DIAG-MACROBETA-0001 crude betas to trade Indian equity direction (operator question 2026-07-23, ">95% accurate" framing). Same family as DIAG-MACROBETA-0001; NOT a new spec family, consumes NO shadow slot.
result: DO-NOT-BUILD (equity overlay); commodity-futures version PARKED behind SPEC-AG-01
notes: OUTCOME-BLIND, NO TRIAL SPENT (cumulative trial count UNCHANGED at 53), NO SHADOW SLOT, no repo outcome data conditioned on any crude signal, no spec drafted, no hash frozen, no broker/Kite call, no scraping (web-search budget exhausted; NO crude source opened this session - all citations marked unverified-at-source). Analysis: analysis/crude_forecast_engine_feasibility.md. FINDINGS: (1) the ">95% accurate" premise is REJECTED - no public crude model achieves it; short-horizon crude direction ~ random walk; a 95% belief = in-sample fit, the free bull-regime "up" call, or hindsight recall (TRAP 4 invented number). (2) DIAG-MACROBETA-0001 betas are EXPOSURE not alpha (its own S11); a tradeable chain needs BOTH (a) crude direction forecastable OVER THE FUTURES CURVE out-of-sample AND (b) survival of transmission net of friction - the macro memo settled only the transmission channel, not (a). (3) The stronger LAGGED crude beta (SPEC-L1 16 survivors vs SPEC-C 6) is NOT predictability - it is the non-synchronous-timing signature (NSE closes 15:30 IST before Brent settles), the stock CATCHING UP to already-public crude news at the next open; C-vs-L1 beta corr only 0.24; the tiny real open-gap opportunity is a latency game, friction-dominated (~8 bps expected gap at beta 0.08 vs ~32 bps round-trip), and NOT ours. (4) DOUBLE TAX: stock captures only beta_oil 0.05-0.11 of the crude move while paying an unscaled ~32 bps round-trip floor (statutory 22.4 bps, ~89% irreducible STT, per costs_in.py/RULING 5, + 5 bps/side slippage); break-even needs a NET directional crude move of 2.95% (HINDOILEXP) to 6.23% (OIL) per round trip - ~1.5-2 sigma of EXPECTED daily crude move - one-to-two orders of magnitude above any plausible edge. (5) A crude view is STRICTLY BETTER expressed in crude futures (beta=1, no attenuation) = SPEC-AG-01 commodity territory, not equity. (6) Literature (Alquist-Kilian-Vigfusson; Baumeister-Kilian real-time; Driesprong-Jacobsen-Maat index-level oil->stock UNDER-REACTION, contested OOS; oil-beta-priced debate MIXED; India oil-equity thin) - all UNVERIFIED AT SOURCE this session, directions only, no numbers quoted. LEGITIMATE USE of the betas = risk management/hedging/factor-neutralisation + conditioning input for an existing macro view (crude vol IS forecastable; crude DIRECTION is not). Narrow defensible path = crude/curve OOS forecasting in the commodity itself, scored vs curve+random-walk, weekly/monthly, in AG-01 space behind AG-01 blockers - requires drafted spec + pre-stated null + hash-freeze + a shadow slot (BOTH HELD) + a registered SPA-gated trial; THIS MEMO AUTHORIZES NONE OF IT.

description: Tier-1 forward monitoring look: 3-session forward returns (close 2026-07-20 -> close 2026-07-23) of the 250-name legacy universe juxtaposed AGAINST the DIAG-MACROBETA-0001 dev-window crude betas; qualitative reconciliation only, no performance statistic computed
result: LOOK-DON'T-TUNE (RULING 2), NOT a sealed-test trial; NO shadow slot, NO sealed test burned; cumulative DSR/SPA trial count UNCHANGED at 53 (see AMENDMENT-A adjudication in notes)
notes: PERFORMED THIS SESSION 2026-07-23 (a Tier-1 forward glance, not an authored trial). GOVERNANCE FRAMING: this observes TIER-1 FORWARD data (post-2026-06-29 live window), NOT the sealed holdout (cutoff 2024-07-17) that AMENDMENT A and the trial budget protect. yfinance AUTO-ADJUSTED public data only (BINDING RULE 3 compliant, no Kite, no broker call, no scraping). Universe = the 250 names in the sanctioned sealed backup data/sealed/raw/2026-07-23/prices_eod_backup_20260624.csv (250 rows), NOT production config.py. HARD BOUNDARY HELD: no portfolio, no strategy/spread/basket return, no cumulative performance, no Sharpe, no IR, no hit-rate, no forward-return-conditional STATISTIC; no names selected, tuned, weighted or ranked; FINAL_TEST NOT set. || THE LOOK || Of the 250-name universe, 66 ROSE over the 3 sessions close 2026-07-20 -> close 2026-07-23; 184 fell/flat. The 66 gainers were intersected with the crude-directional names from DIAG-MACROBETA-0001 (a-priori sign map = 52 crude-directional names in governance/prereg/DIAG-MACROBETA-0001_sign_map.csv; empirical FDR survivors = 18 via C_oil_bh_reject / L1_oil_bh_reject in data/derived/macro_beta_diag/betas.parquet; union pool = 61). 16 crude names were among the 66 gainers. || MACRO BACKDROP (reliable) || Brent (BZ=F), WTI (CL=F), USD/INR (INR=X) pulled over the same window: crude rose ~+11.7% 2026-07-16 -> 2026-07-22 (Brent 84.23 -> 94.07, WTI corroborating); USD/INR essentially FLAT (~+0.3%). || DATA-QUALITY CATCH (recorded as PART of the diagnostic's value, TRAP 6 silence / TRAP 8 estimator hygiene) || The 2026-07-23 crude bars are CORRUPTED and were DISCARDED, not used: Brent printed -7.4% (94.07 -> 87.10) while WTI printed +6.4% (86.83 -> 92.43) on the SAME day - a ~14% same-day DECOUPLING of two grades whose daily log returns co-move at ~0.89 (DIAG-MACROBETA-0001 S3), physically impossible, almost certainly an unsettled/partial-session yfinance artifact (same failure shape as TRAP 6: a healthy-looking bar that is actually a partial session). The reliable crude read stops at 2026-07-22. || RECONCILIATION (QUALITATIVE ONLY - WEAK, SPLIT, PARTIAL) || CONSISTENT with crude-up: E&P / crude-POSITIVE names rose (ONGC +1.1%, OIL +1.2%, VEDL +1.1%), and crude-NEGATIVE demand/margin names fell (OMCs HINDPETRO -5.0% / BPCL -2.4% / IOC -1.7%, aviation INDIGO -3.9%). INCONSISTENT: the FDR-significant crude-NEGATIVE input-cost names rose slightly (PIDILITIND +0.6%, BERGEPAINT +0.6%, DALBHARAT +0.4%) - i.e. the very names that carried the dev-window crude signal did NOT track it over these 3 days. IRRELEVANT: the headline movers (autos +7-9%: M&MFIN, TVSMOTOR, BAJAJ-AUTO) have statistically INSIGNIFICANT crude betas, so their moves say nothing about crude. INTERPRETIVE DISCIPLINE: 3 sessions against a beta estimated on 2,353 sessions confirms NOTHING about the beta and is fully consistent with CHANCE ALIGNMENT; this look does NOT re-test, revalidate, refute or update DIAG-MACROBETA-0001. || AMENDMENT-A ADJUDICATION (do not paper over - argue both sides) || FOR 'it is a look, not a sealed-test trial': it is TIER-1 FORWARD data (post-2026-06-29), NOT the sealed holdout (cutoff 2024-07-17); no strategy/spread/portfolio return, Sharpe, IR or hit-rate was computed; no names were selected or tuned; no family's single sealed test and no shadow slot were touched; RULING 2 (governance/DECISIONS.md 2026-07-17) explicitly permits look-don't-tune on Tier-1 forward data. AGAINST 'it edges toward a conditional observation': it DID observe forward returns of signal-classified (crude-beta) names and asked whether they lined up - the exact shape AMENDMENT A names ('conditional return plots ... sanity-check performance summaries ... regardless of formality'). DETERMINATION: recorded as a TIER-1 LOOK-DON'T-TUNE DIAGNOSTIC that consumes NO shadow slot and burns NO family's sealed test. On the shape-match to AMENDMENT A, it lands on the LOOK side: AMENDMENT A and the DSR/SPA cumulative-trial count exist to charge the DEV/HOLDOUT multiple-testing budget (RULING 7 - best-of-N selection over dev/sealed data); a forward monitoring glance with NO performance statistic and NO selection does not draw on that budget. CUMULATIVE DSR/SPA TRIAL COUNT UNCHANGED AT 53 (count the file: this row makes 15 data rows / 14 prior + this; the trial COUNT is a separate ledger from the row count and is not incremented by a look). HONEST RESIDUAL (not a false all-clear, TRAP 6): the ONE point on which an operator could take a more conservative view is whether AMENDMENT A's 'regardless of formality' should reach a no-statistic forward glance; if so the conservative treatment is to charge ONE marginal trial (-> 54), which per TRIAL ECONOMICS is CHEAP (moves the deflation bar ~0.3%) and changes NOTHING in this result. Flagged for operator override; nothing here is gated on it. || LOOK-DON'T-TUNE FIREWALL (binding) || This observation MUST NOT feed the design, selection or tuning of any research family; if it ever does, it becomes contamination and a registered trial. A green 3-day alignment is not evidence for or against any strategy and grants no re-test of DIAG-MACROBETA-0001 or any spec family.

description: 3-day up-vs-down cross-sectional description (close 2026-07-20 -> close 2026-07-23), 250-name legacy universe: 66 up / 184 down/flat; base-rate-controlled sector/size/beta/crude-fx/liquidity cross-tab. result: LOOK-DON'T-TUNE (RULING 2): sector rotation on a DOWN tape; no per-stock trigger recoverable
notes: NO TRIAL SPENT (outcome-blind bucket: up-vs-down cross-sectional DESCRIPTION with mandatory base-rate control; no strategy return, Sharpe, IR, hit-rate or forward-return-conditional statistic beyond the descriptive split — AMENDMENT A 'correlation structure' reasoning, same basis as DIAG-MACROBETA-0001-FWDLOOK). FINDINGS: the tape FELL over the window (universe mean -1.29%, median -1.41%), so '66 rose' is NOT high-beta-on-an-up-tape — the up-group has LOWER dev-window market beta (0.951 vs 0.991), up-rate declines across beta terciles (0.30/0.30/0.21), Spearman(ret,beta) = -0.13. SURVIVED the base-rate control (vs 26.4% base up-rate): Chemicals 62.5% (5/8, small-N), Metals&Mining 58.3% (7/12), Autos 42.1% (8/19); mild large-cap tilt; mild higher delivery-pct (stale snapshot). WASHED OUT (equally common in losers): crude-beta clustering (+0.0017 up vs +0.0016 down — confirms the FWDLOOK finding independently), turnover, high-beta momentum, Capital Goods/Power/FMCG/Healthcare. BASE-RATE LESSON (now TRAP 10): Financials supplied the MOST winners by raw count (10) yet had the LOWEST up-rate of any large sector — counting winners without the control mislabels it a theme. VERDICT: sector rotation (commodity complex + autos) on a risk-off tape; NO recoverable per-stock trigger; all news narratives labelled POST-HOC/UNVERIFIED/NOT USABLE; the operator's pre-named 8-stock auto list (8/8 up) is operator-selected, not independent evidence. CAVEATS: small-N sectors; market proxy = universe mean (sealed nifty backup is one stale day); betas/liquidity are pre-cutoff/stale descriptors; 41/250 names lack a dev-window beta. FIREWALL: monitoring only — this observation must NEVER feed the design, selection or tuning of any research family, spec or signal; any downstream use that selects or tunes on it retroactively becomes a TRIAL and must be registered as such.
